title: Iced Peppery Dutch Beef Roast
description: A velvety, peppery roast with a chilled glaze, perfect for a comforting sick‑day Dutch meal.
instructions: Pat the beef cutlet dry with paper towels and season both sides with kosher salt, fresh black pepper, and smoked paprika.
Heat the olive oil in a heavy Dutch oven over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Sear the seasoned beef for 3-4 minutes on each side until a dark brown crust forms.
Remove the beef from the pot and set aside; add the sliced onion and minced garlic to the same pot and cook until the onion softens, about 2 minutes.
Pour in the beef broth and red wine, stirring to loosen any browned bits from the pan.
Return the beef to the pot, cover, reduce the heat to low, and simmer gently for 45 minutes until the meat is tender.
Transfer the beef to a cutting board, cover with foil, and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Whisk the honey and water together, then add the mixture to the reduced sauce, stirring until it thickens into a glossy glaze.
Spread the chilled glaze over the sliced beef and refrigerate for 30 minutes to achieve an iced finish.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving.
